title: Cybersecurity in the Age of Instant Software

metadata.title: Cybersecurity in the Age of Instant Software
metadata.description: AI is rapidly changing how software is written, deployed, and used. Trends point to a future where AIs can write custom software quickly and easily: “instant software.” Taken to an extreme, it might become easier for a user to have an AI write an application on demand—a spreadsheet, for example—and delete it when you’re done using it than to buy one commercially. Future systems could include a mix: both traditional long-term software and ephemeral instant software that is constantly being written, deployed, modified, and deleted. AI is changing cybersecurity as well. In particular, AI systems are getting better at finding and patching vulnerabilities in code. This has implications for both attackers and defenders, depending on the ways this and related technologies improve...

title: New Mexico’s Meta Ruling and Encryption

One of the key pieces of evidence the New Mexico attorney general used against Meta was the company’s 2023 decision to add end-to-end encryption to Facebook Messenger. The argument went like this: predators used Messenger to groom minors and exchange child sexual abuse material. metadata.description: WebinarTV searches the internet for public Zoom invites, joins the meetings, secretly records them, and publishes (alternate link ) the recordings. It doesn’t use the Zoom record feature, so Zoom can’t do anything about it.

title: US Bans All Foreign-Made Consumer Routers

metadata.title: US Bans All Foreign-Made Consumer Routers
metadata.description: This is for new routers ; you don’t have to throw away your existing ones: The Executive Branch determination noted that foreign-produced routers (1) introduce “a supply chain vulnerability that could disrupt the U.S. economy, critical infrastructure, and national defense” and (2) pose “a severe cybersecurity risk that could be leveraged to immediately and severely disrupt U.S. critical infrastructure and directly harm U.S. persons.” More information : Any new router made outside the US will now need to be approved by the FCC before it can be imported, marketed, or sold in the country...

title: Possible US Government iPhone Hacking Tool Leaked

metadata.title: Possible US Government iPhone Hacking Tool Leaked
metadata.description: Wired writes (alternate source ): Security researchers at Google on Tuesday released a report describing what they’re calling “Coruna,” a highly sophisticated iPhone hacking toolkit that includes five complete hacking techniques capable of bypassing all the defenses of an iPhone to silently install malware on a device when it visits a website containing the exploitation code. In total, Coruna takes advantage of 23 distinct vulnerabilities in iOS, a rare collection of hacking components that suggests it was created by a well-resourced, likely state-sponsored group of hackers...

title: Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records

metadata.title: Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
metadata.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger

metadata.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
metadata.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms

metadata.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
metadata.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise

metadata.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
metadata.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras

metadata.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
metadata.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version

metadata.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
metadata.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning

metadata.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
metadata.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers

metadata.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
metadata.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days

metadata.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
metadata.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year

metadata.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
metadata.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.

title: Apple Intelligence AI Guardrails Bypassed in New Attack

metadata.title: Apple Intelligence AI Guardrails Bypassed in New Attack
metadata.description: RSAC researchers hacked Apple Intelligence using the Neural Exect method and Unicode manipulation. The post Apple Intelligence AI Guardrails Bypassed in New Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek .

title: Can we Trust AI? No – But Eventually We Must

metadata.title: Can we Trust AI? No – But Eventually We Must
metadata.description: From hallucinations and bias to model collapse and adversarial abuse, today’s AI is built on probability rather than truth, yet enterprises are deploying it at speed without fully understanding the risks. The post Can we Trust AI? No – But Eventually We Must appeared first on SecurityWeek .

title: Google API Keys in Android Apps Expose Gemini Endpoints to Unauthorized Access

metadata.title: Google API Keys in Android Apps Expose Gemini Endpoints to Unauthorized Access
metadata.description: Dozens of such keys can be extracted from apps’ decompiled code to gain access to all Gemini endpoints. The post Google API Keys in Android Apps Expose Gemini Endpoints to Unauthorized Access appeared first on SecurityWeek .

title: Palo Alto Networks, SonicWall Patch High-Severity Vulnerabilities

metadata.title: Palo Alto Networks, SonicWall Patch High-Severity Vulnerabilities
metadata.description: The bugs could allow attackers to modify protected resources and escalate their privileges to administrator. The post Palo Alto Networks, SonicWall Patch High-Severity Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek .

title: The Hidden ROI of Visibility: Better Decisions, Better Behavior, Better Security

metadata.title: The Hidden ROI of Visibility: Better Decisions, Better Behavior, Better Security
metadata.description: Beyond monitoring and compliance, visibility acts as a powerful deterrent, shaping user behavior, improving collaboration, and enabling more accurate, data-driven security decisions. The post The Hidden ROI of Visibility: Better Decisions, Better Behavior, Better Security appeared first on SecurityWeek .

title: Google Warns of New Campaign Targeting BPOs to Steal Corporate Data

metadata.title: Google Warns of New Campaign Targeting BPOs to Steal Corporate Data
metadata.description: Tracked as UNC6783, the threat actor is likely linked to Mr. Raccoon, the hacker behind the alleged theft of Adobe data from a BPO. The post Google Warns of New Campaign Targeting BPOs to Steal Corporate Data appeared first on SecurityWeek .

title: Adobe Reader Zero-Day Exploited for Months: Researcher

metadata.title: Adobe Reader Zero-Day Exploited for Months: Researcher
metadata.description: Reputable researcher Haifei Li has come across what appears to be a PDF designed to exploit an unpatched vulnerability. The post Adobe Reader Zero-Day Exploited for Months: Researcher appeared first on SecurityWeek .

title: 300,000 People Impacted by Eurail Data Breach

metadata.title: 300,000 People Impacted by Eurail Data Breach
metadata.description: In December 2025, hackers stole names and passport numbers from the European travel company’s network. The post 300,000 People Impacted by Eurail Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek .

title: $3.6 Million Stolen in Bitcoin Depot Hack

metadata.title: $3.6 Million Stolen in Bitcoin Depot Hack
metadata.description: A hacker transferred more than 50 bitcoin from the Bitcoin ATM operator’s wallets after stealing credentials. The post $3.6 Million Stolen in Bitcoin Depot Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek .

title: Shaky Ceasefire Unlikely to Stop Cyberattacks From Iran-Linked Hackers for Long

metadata.title: Shaky Ceasefire Unlikely to Stop Cyberattacks From Iran-Linked Hackers for Long
metadata.description: Hackers vowed to revive its efforts against America when the time was right — demonstrating how digital warfare has become ingrained in military conflict. The post Shaky Ceasefire Unlikely to Stop Cyberattacks From Iran-Linked Hackers for Long appeared first on SecurityWeek .
